summary: data-parser-utils ships install/load-time shell execution via bash/zsh history harvest pattern
details: index.js imports child_process at the top of the module and invokes execSync against bash and zsh at lines 301 and 317. The shape — require('child_process') at module top with execSync('bash...') and execSync('zsh...') calls reachable from the main entry — matches the shell-history harvest fingerprint (reading ~/.bash_history / ~/.zsh_history or running history/fc -l under a login shell and exfiltrating the output). Shell history routinely contains credentials, tokens, and connection strings, so collecting and shipping it off-host is credential theft on the installer's machine regardless of how the package frames itself. The package name ('data-parser-utils') has no plausible reason to spawn bash and zsh subshells. The traced code content also tripped the malware-output safety filter, which independently corroborates that the contents read as operational credential-theft code rather than as a benign data-parsing utility.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
